bbc.title: Russian soldiers tell BBC they saw fellow troops executed on commanders' orders
bbc.description: Four men expose the horror and brutality of conditions in the Ukraine war, with two saying they saw soldiers being shot for refusing orders.

bbc.title: Mexico sends thousands of soldiers to stop violence after death of drug lord
bbc.description: A wave of violence has erupted across Mexico since a powerful drug cartel boss died following his capture by special forces.

bbc.title: More than 5,000 flights cancelled as US east coast digs out of record snow
bbc.description: A major snowstorm dumps more than a foot of snow in several areas, bringing power cuts and disruption.

bbc.title: French minister moves to block US envoy Kushner from government access
bbc.description: The minister says Charles Kushner, father of Trump's son-in-law Jared, failed to explain comments about violence in France.

bbc.title: Robert Carradine, Lizzie McGuire and Revenge of the Nerds star, dies aged 71
bbc.description: His family says the actor took his own life after living with bipolar disorder for nearly two decades.

bbc.title: Celebrity doctor Peter Attia steps down from CBS over Epstein links
bbc.description: The health influencer's spokesman says he wants to avoid his association with the late sex offender becoming "a distraction".

bbc.title: Probe under way after illness kills 72 tigers in Thai tourist park
bbc.description: Authorities say samples showed the canine distemper virus, but have not confirmed where it came from.

bbc.title: US partially evacuates Beirut embassy amid rising Iran tensions
bbc.description: The State Department has ordered non-essential staff to leave the embassy in Beirut after a security review.

wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
